story.text: GitHub must be only a deterministic reflection of local truth (bdo, 2026-06-18), and the pens prove it by being write-through carbon copies. The merge-node's landing receipt broke that: it recorded THAT a PR landed (pr, epic, head) but not WHICH atoms reached main, so the pipeline namespace (per-atom) and the git-merge namespace (per-PR) could not join, and the loop could never confirm a judged-good piece was actually on main (the terminal-pull gateway's namespace gap, done-line 0123). This is the fix: the doctrine gains D-13 (external surfaces are deterministic reflections; the writing pen carries enough to re-derive the local truth it reflects), and the merge receipt carries landed_atoms — the artifact_ids the PR's range adds, computed by reusing the same _range_atom_facts reach the off-log gate uses, so the receipt cannot disagree with what the gate counted. A pure join fold (digest.atoms_on_main) reads them, so 'did atom X reach main?' is answerable from the log alone. Value: the merge stops being a lossy summary and becomes a faithful carbon copy of local truth; the two namespaces join going forward; and the terminal-pull gateway gains a real signal to drain on (its passed-but-unpulled patrol becomes computable). Phase A of bdo's two-phase direction — Phase B (the pipeline seams updated with the gateway implementations) depends on this join and is its own later arc.
